channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Cross-Generational Voices Discuss Nuclear Disarmament and Anti-War Strategies in Japan-Korea-US Policy
item.description: The 'Nuclear Bombs Never Again' webinar, scheduled for August 4th at 5 pm Pacific Time (8 pm Eastern Time / 9 am Japan Standard Time), will bring together cross-generational voices from Japan, South K...

item.title: Man Jailed for Life After Shaking Four-Week-Old Son in Alcohol-Fueled Outburst
item.description: Tony Bartlett, 39, was sentenced to life imprisonment with a minimum term of 18 years after violently shaking his four-week-old son Atticus to death. The incident occurred on July 16, 2022, when Bartl...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Deliberates on Suliman Carrim's Testimony Delay
item.description: The Madlanga Commission is reviewing whether North West businessman Suliman Carrim should be granted an indefinite postponement from testifying due to alleged health issues. Evidence presented suggest...

item.title: Conservative Criticizes Todd Blanche's Appointment as Unfit for Attorney General Role
item.description: George Will, a respected conservative Washington Post columnist, publicly condemned the potential confirmation of Todd Blanche as Attorney General, criticizing the Trump administration's appointee for...

item.title: Benjamin Mkapa's Leadership in Tanzania's Economic Transition
item.description: Benjamin Mkapa, a pivotal figure in Tanzanian history, navigated the country through a challenging economic transition during his presidency from 1995 to 2005. Born in 1938 in Lupaso, Tanzania, Mkapa'...

item.title: US Conducts Military Strikes on Iran Amid Red Sea Shipping Tensions
item.description: The United States launched missile strikes across Iran, reaching as far as the Caspian coast, following President Donald Trump's threats of 'major military punishment' against Tehran and its Houthi al...

item.title: Edo State Advances Universal Healthcare Through SHINE Agenda
item.description: The Edo State Government remains committed to strengthening primary healthcare as a key strategy for achieving Universal Health Coverage (UHC) and improving health outcomes for residents. At the Edo S...

item.title: Wamalwa supports Sifuna in Western Kenya political strategy against Ruto
item.description: DAP-K leader Eugene Wamalwa has clarified his stance on Nairobi Senator Edwin Sifuna's growing influence in Western Kenya politics. During a radio interview on July 24, Wamalwa rejected claims that Si...

item.title: Guelph plant business faces significant theft loss
item.description: A local plant business in Guelph is dealing with a major theft incident, with thousands of dollars in assets reported missing. The incident has raised concerns among local authorities and business own...

item.title: MEA clarifies: Monsoon rainfall, not India, caused Pakistan's flood issues
item.description: The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) has denied allegations that India caused flooding in Pakistan by releasing river waters. In a statement, MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal highlighted that Pakist...

item.title: Teenager convicted in 2024 Georgia high school shooting that killed four
item.description: A 16-year-old boy has pleaded guilty to 55 charges related to a deadly shooting at Apalachee High School in Georgia, USA, which occurred on September 4, 2024. The incident resulted in the deaths of tw...

item.title: IHC Registrar Directs Justice Babar Sattar to Vacate Islamabad Residence
item.description: The Islamabad High Court (IHC) registrar has issued a notice to Justice Babar Sattar, directing him to vacate his Islamabad residence within 30 days following his transfer to the Peshawar High Court (...

item.title: Chris Affleck Passes Away at 83 After Pancreatic Cancer Diagnosis
item.description: Chris Anne Affleck, mother of Hollywood stars Ben and Casey Affleck, died peacefully in her sleep on June 2, 2025, at the age of 83 after a prolonged battle with pancreatic cancer. Diagnosed in Decemb...

item.title: Gombe Police Recover Stolen Pistol and Vehicle After Investigation
item.description: The Gombe State Police Command successfully recovered a Beretta pistol and a stolen Toyota Camry following two separate operations. The pistol, found hidden under a stone near Brave Rock Sport and Rec...

item.title: WestJet introduces flexible booking options amid potential flight attendant strike
item.description: WestJet has announced a new flexible travel policy for customers planning to fly between July 30 and August 4, in response to ongoing negotiations with its flight attendants' union. The airline warns ...

item.title: Rising Inflation Drives Shift to Home-Bought Frozen Desserts
item.description: A new study reveals that 72% of American adults prefer keeping frozen treats at home to save money compared to dining out, with summer heat and cost concerns driving this trend. The National Frozen & ...

item.title: DLSU Students Promote Media Literacy via Campus Patrol Initiative
item.description: DLSU students are leveraging the Campus Patrol program to enhance media literacy and engage in media production, as highlighted in a recent article. The initiative, part of a broader effort to educate...

item.title: Good Weekend Quiz and Crossword Challenge Offers Mental Stimulation

item.title: Winnipeg reports decline in fires linked to homelessness factors in 2026
item.description: A report from Winnipeg indicates a notable reduction in fires during 2026, with authorities attributing this trend to the influence of homelessness as a contributing factor. The analysis suggests that...

item.title: Government Adjusts Fuel Prices Amid Global Oil Market Fluctuations
item.description: The government has raised petrol prices by Rs3.66 and high-speed diesel (HSD) by Rs4.80 per litre, reflecting global oil price volatility due to renewed US-Iran tensions in the Persian Gulf. Petrol no...
